title: FailureMem: A Failure-Aware Multimodal Framework for Autonomous Software Repair
what_happened: arXiv:2603.17826v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Multimodal Automated Program Repair (MAPR) extends traditional program repair by requiring models to jointly reason over source code, textual issue descriptions, and visual artifacts such as GUI screenshots. title: **Introducing SPEED-Bench: A Unified and Diverse Benchmark for Speculative Decoding**
what_happened: Hugging Face Blog announced '**Introducing SPEED-Bench: A Unified and Diverse Benchmark for Speculative Decoding**', highlighting new developments relevant to AI researchers and practitioners.

title: OpenAI overhauls ChatGPT's model selection
what_happened: OpenAI has redesigned how model selection works in ChatGPT. The article OpenAI overhauls ChatGPT's model selection appeared first on The Decoder.

title: Elevenlabs now lets you sell AI music you don't own
what_happened: Elevenlabs has launched a marketplace for AI-generated music that pays creators when their tracks get downloaded or licensed. But who actually owns the music? A look at the terms of use tells the story: no one, really. The article Elevenlabs now lets you sell AI music you don't own appeared first on The Decoder.

title: Visa prepares payment systems for AI agent-initiated transactions
what_happened: Payments rely on a simple model: a person decides to buy something, and a bank or card network processes the transaction. That model is starting to change as Visa tests how AI agents can initiate payments. New work in the banking sector suggests that, in some cases, software agents may soon take on that role.
